fix(profile): clean up navigation — each item leads to distinct screen (#533)

Fix Profile navigation structure by consolidating duplicate Settings routes

Problem: Multiple Profile menu items (rest timer, weight unit, training phase,
notifications, health integration, export data, clear data) all routed to the
same Settings screen, creating a confusing user experience where different-
sounding options led to the same destination.

Solution: Replaced the Preferences and Data sections (7 items total) with a
single Settings menu item. This provides a clearer navigation structure where
each Profile menu item leads to a distinct destination:
- Talk to Coach → Coach screen
- Progress → Progress screen
- Analytics → Analytics screen
- Exercise Library → Exercise Library screen
- Tools → Tools screen
- Recovery → Recovery screen
- Settings → Settings screen (consolidated)

The individual preference options (rest timer, weight unit, etc.) are still
accessible within the Settings screen where they belong.

Changes:
- Consolidated 7 Settings-bound menu items into 1 Settings item in ProfileScreen.kt
- Added bilingual strings (EN/ES) for profile_settings and profile_settings_subtitle
- Build verified: assembleDebug passes successfully

commit.message:
Add TV Display page with remote dashboard controls (#21)

Splits TV player features out of PR #16, building on top of the existing
montage player. Adds a full-screen `/:token/tv` page for projecting on a
TV, controlled remotely from the dashboard via `useSyncedState`.

### New: TV Display (`tv-display.tsx`, `tv-player.tsx`)

- Full-viewport ReactPlayer at `/:token/tv`, no native controls
- Reads `activeSong`, `tvPlayback`, `tvMode` synced state; writes
`tvProgress` back
- Song overlay (flag/country/artist) fades in/out over 4s on song change
- Auto-advances to next song on `onEnded`; montage mode plays via
`ESC_MONTAGE_DATA[escYear]`
- Montage mode tracks active song from timestamps and syncs it back to
dashboard via `activeSong`
- Uses callback ref pattern for proper YouTube element initialization
- Standby screen when idle

### Dashboard as pure remote control

The dashboard renders no video — it acts as a pure remote control for
what the TV displays.

- **TV playback controls**: play/pause toggle, skip (⏭), progress bar
reading `tvProgress`
- **Montage toggle** sets `tvMode("montage")` to tell TV to play the
montage (no inline player)
- **Montage prev/next** (⏮/⏭) seek to previous/next song timestamp in
the montage video
- Song clicks set `tvMode("song")` so TV starts playing the individual
video
- "Now playing" bar shows current song in real time (updated by TV
during montage via synced state)
- Duration shown per song button via `formatDuration()`
- "📺 Open TV Display" link opens TV page in new tab
- Songs grouped by semifinal halves (from main)

### Synced state channels

Four channels coordinate dashboard ↔ TV communication:

| Channel | Direction | Shape |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| `activeSong` | dashboard ↔ TV | `string \| null` (country name; TV
writes during montage) |
| `tvMode` | dashboard ↔ TV | `"song" \| "montage" \| null` |
| `tvPlayback` | dashboard → TV | `{ command: "play" \| "pause" \|
"seek", seekTo?: number }` |
| `tvProgress` | TV → dashboard | `{ playedFraction, playedSeconds,
loadedFraction }` |

### E2E tests (11 new tests)

- TV standby screen shown when no song is active
- TV shows "Game not found" for invalid tokens
- Dashboard has "📺 Open TV Display" link
- Dashboard song controls: play/pause toggle, skip, stop
- Dashboard montage toggle shows/hides montage playing state
- Dashboard montage prev/next buttons appear during montage playback
- Dashboard ↔ TV synced state: song selection makes TV leave standby,
stop returns TV to standby
- Song duration displayed in song buttons
- Songs grouped by semifinal halves

### No database changes

Uses existing `escYear` + `ESC_MONTAGE_DATA` lookup instead of adding a
`montageYoutubeId` column.

R_IsLightSource matched entities with id == 0 against lights with
source == 0, causing all entities (and their linked children) with

prevented weapons, CTF flags, and other linked models on players

commit.message:
Fix WebSocket protocol: mirror page protocol (http→ws, https→wss)

When the server runs with TLS certs the page is served over https://.
The hardcoded ws:// endpoint caused mixed-content blocks and CORS
failures because browsers reject http WebSocket connections from an
https page.

Fix: derive the WebSocket protocol from window.location.protocol so
https pages use wss:// and http pages keep ws://.

fix: configure git identity in release action before tagging (#7471)

The 'Create git tag' step was failing with 'Committer identity unknown' because
git identity was only configured in the conditional 'Commit version update' step.
When that step was skipped (no version changes), git identity was never set.

Now git config is called directly in the 'Create git tag' step to ensure
git identity is always available for annotated tag creation.

commit.message:
Add HTTPS support for LAN play (WebGPU requires secure context)

WebGPU is only available in secure contexts. Plain http:// on a LAN IP
does not qualify, so navigator.gpu is undefined and self.GPUShaderStage
is undefined, crashing the renderer with 'Vx is undefined / VERTEX'.

Server changes:
- Auto-detect server/certs/cert.pem + key.pem at startup
- If present: start HTTPS server (wss:// for Colyseus WebSocket too)
- If absent: start HTTP with a console warning pointing to setup script
- CORS simplified to allow all origins (LAN, localhost, http/https)

New script: scripts/setup-certs.sh
- Requires mkcert (brew/apt/choco install mkcert)
- Installs a local CA trusted by the OS/browser (no cert warnings)
- Generates cert for localhost + current LAN IP
- Writes to server/certs/ (gitignored)
- Run once; restart the server to pick up HTTPS

commit.message:
fix: new session sorts to bottom, photo button dismisses keyboard

1. Set initial session preview on session_created so new sessions have a
   timestamp and sort to the top of the list instead of falling to the
   bottom behind sessions with activity.

2. Replace image upload <label htmlFor> with a <button onClick> that
   triggers fileInputRef.click() directly. The native label-to-input
   association caused the textarea to blur, dismissing the iOS keyboard
   with a jarring layout jump before the file picker appeared.

Bump arm/web to 0.10.3.
